lines.text: Phoenix file 62-213
In explaining the results of photographing the ship, Mr. [REDACTED]
stated that he was surprised that the object appeared dark on a light back-
ground; that he fully expected that the object would be light on a dark back-
ground. He described the light spot which appears in the center of the object
(Exhibit II) as being a green house. [REDACTED] insisted that the ship made no
noise that was audible to his ear and he was unable to detect the presence of b7c
any propeller. He also related that after the newspaper release over Radio
Station KTAR, wherein statements were made that he, [REDACTED], had stated Army
officials were studying the photographs, and that it was a top secret, he
attempted to determine the source of this release and the Radio station finally
told him that it was a United Press release from Washington, D. C.
The following description of [REDACTED] was obtained from observation b7c
and interrogation:
[REDACTED] b7c
It should be stated that at the time of interview with [REDACTED] Mr.
[REDACTED] identified himself as an agent of the Federal Bureau of Investigation and
exhibited his credentials. However, pursuant to a request from Mr. [REDACTED], he
was introduced only as a representative of the United States government. His
exact official connection was not made known to Mr. [REDACTED]. Agent [REDACTED] advised
that he felt it was a peculiar procedure, but it was not his business. However,
at the conclusion of the interview, Mr. [REDACTED] requested Mr. [REDACTED] to furnish
him with the negatives and [REDACTED] stated he would be glad to do so, but they b7c
were not in his immediate possession, and he would deliver them to the FBI the
following morning. Mr. [REDACTED] requested that the negatives be returned to him.
Agent [REDACTED] states that to this request, Mr. [REDACTED] made no reply. Agent [REDACTED]
states that after leaving Mr. [REDACTED] he asked [REDACTED] if the negatives would be
returned to Mr. [REDACTED] and [REDACTED] stated that he doubted very much that they would
be returned. Mr. [REDACTED] then advised Mr. [REDACTED] that before he, [REDACTED], would
accept these negatives that [REDACTED] must be advised of Mr. [REDACTED] identity and
also advised that the negatives, if turned over to Mr. [REDACTED], would not be
returned to him.
